title: Boiling Water
immediate_steps: Filter the water through a cloth to remove large debris
Bring the water to a rolling boil over a fire or stove
Maintain the rolling boil for at least 1 minute (3 minutes at high altitudes above 6,500 feet)
Let the water cool naturally before drinking
Store in clean containers
avoid: Do not assume clear water is safe
Do not shorten the boiling time

title: Chemical Purification (Iodine/Bleach)
symptoms: Have access to household bleach or iodine tablets
immediate_steps: Filter water through a cloth to remove sediment
For liquid bleach (unscented, 5-9% sodium hypochlorite): add 2 drops per quart (liter) of water
Stir and let stand for 30 minutes. Water should have a slight chlorine odor
For iodine tablets: follow package instructions (usually 2 tablets per quart)
Let sit for at least 30 minutes before drinking
avoid: Do not use scented, color-safe, or added-cleaner bleach
Do not use iodine continuously for more than a few weeks

title: Lost In Forest - Water Procurement
immediate_steps: Search for moving water sources such as streams and flowing rivers when available
Purify water before consumption whenever possible by boiling, filtering or using purification tablets
Collect rainwater if available
Reduce unnecessary physical activity to minimize dehydration and conserve body fluids
avoid: Avoid drinking stagnant or contaminated water directly

title: Water Purification - Safe Water Sources
immediate_steps: Prefer flowing water sources such as streams and rivers over stagnant water
Rainwater collected directly is generally safer than standing water
Water from ponds and stagnant pools must be purified before use
avoid: Avoid water sources containing unusual color, odor, foam or visible contamination

title: Water Purification - Boiling Method
symptoms: Need to kill microorganisms in water
immediate_steps: Bring water to a rolling boil and maintain it for at least one minute
In high altitude areas, boil for at least three minutes
Allow the water to cool naturally before drinking
Store boiled water in a clean container to avoid recontamination
avoid: Boiling does not remove chemical pollutants (only effective against bacteria, viruses, parasites)

title: Water Purification - Filtration Methods
symptoms: Water is dirty or suspended particles are present
immediate_steps: Filter water using cloth, sand, gravel, or commercial filters
Create a simple improvised filter by layering cloth, charcoal, sand, and small stones
After filtration, water should be boiled if possible for complete purification
avoid: Avoid drinking directly filtered water from unknown sources without boiling (filtration does not guarantee removal of pathogens)

title: Water Purification - Rainwater and Storage Safety
immediate_steps: Use clean containers to collect rainwater
Always store water in covered containers to prevent dust, insects and bacteria from entering
It is recommended to boil or filter rainwater before drinking for maximum safety
avoid: Avoid contamination from roofs or dirty surfaces during collection
